channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: The Pacific is full of diversity but some people want to make themselves whiter
item.description: Skin bleaching and lightening products are widely sold in Tonga, which is part of a region with a long history of colourism.
item.dc:creator: Alice Lolohea

media:group.media:description: Products like this one continue to be sold in Tonga.

item.title: Leaked data shows NT diphtheria cases surged a month before alert was issued
item.description: Internal NT Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Hathaway-Wilson

media:group.media:description: Leaked data reveals diphtheria case numbers surged well before a public health alert was issued by the department.

item.title: Hopes pinned on promising sequel to prevent a Whyalla wipeout
item.description: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.
item.dc:creator: James Wakelin

media:group.media:description: The Whyalla community is hopeful of a promising new future for the steelworks.

item.title: The simple, ordinary joy of Kylie Minogue
item.description: In the end, a woman who's had wins and losses, terrors and triumphs, and who regularly goes back to stay with family in suburban Melbourne, has become a music legend of her time, and it's not hard to figure out why.
item.dc:creator: Virginia Trioli

media:group.media:description: It's not hard to figure out why Kylie Minogue has become one of the music legends of her time.

item.title: Big-wave surfing 'pioneer', 43, dies at remote NSW break, doing what he loved
item.description: Tributes mount for Nathan Bartlett, who chased epic waves around the world but died at a rugged spot near Jervis Bay, not far from where he grew up, on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Nathan Bartlett with his daughter.

item.title: De Minaur and Djokovic bundled out of French Open leaving draw wide open
item.description: Seeded stars continue to tumble at Roland-Garros as both Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic's campaigns end in the third round.

media:group.media:description: Alex de Minaur (pictured) was bundled out of the French Open by Czech teen Jakub Mensik despite winning the first set 6-0.

item.title: How IVF could spell doom for feral rabbits
item.description: Scientists are using rabbit IVF and gene-editing technology to crash Australia's feral rabbit population by spreading infertility through female rabbits.
item.dc:creator: Tim Lee

media:group.media:description: Rabbits are considered one of Australia's most destructive invasive pest species.

item.title: David Szalay wonders whether AI could write hockey smut
item.description: The Booker Prize winner sat down at the Sydney Writers' Festival to ponder the threat of AI to fiction, share some recommendations and consider whether his book was really about masculinity.
item.dc:creator: Gina Rushton

media:group.media:description: David Szalay

item.title: Australian frontman for offshore shell companies faces extradition to US
item.description: A Queensland jetski salesman who helped set up offshore shell companies embroiled in scandals involving Mexican drug cartels and North Korean arms dealers faces extradition to the United States on money laundering charges.
item.dc:creator: Grace Tobin and Elise Worthington

media:group.media:description: Ian Taylor on a jetski in a photo posted to Facebook on October 8, 2020.

item.title: 'Perfect storm' for corruption: IOC petition targets Brisbane's 2032 Olympic stadium plan
item.description: Opponents of Brisbane's centrepiece Olympic stadium take their concerns directly to the IOC's headquarters in Switzerland as a critical deadline looms.
item.dc:creator: Tom Hartley

media:group.media:description: A full breakdown of projected costs for the stadium build is yet to be provided.

item.title: Heat during abnormally warm May for eastern states could be here to stay
item.description: Australia's east is coming to the end of an abnormally warm May, and the heat could be here to stay.
item.dc:creator: Tom Saunders

media:group.media:description: The BOM's modelling estimates the chance of "unusually high maximums" above 80 per cent from south-east Queensland to Tasmania along with the west of Western Australia.

item.title: Motel owners say Booking.com has a stranglehold on their businesses
item.description: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, and consumer advocates think Aussie travellers might also have a claim.
item.dc:creator: Michael Atkin and Lucy Kent

media:group.media:description: Booking.com has become increasingly popular with Aussie travellers.

item.title: Former mayor's lawyer describes private prosecution as 'nightmare'
item.description: A private prosecution launched by a businessman in Victoria's Hepburn Shire has been described as a "nightmare" by a lawyer defending one of two local government figures facing charges.

media:group.media:description: David Penman has brought the charges against the pair under local government laws.

item.title: First of five survivors rescued from flooded Laos cave
item.description: Rescue divers in Laos have safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

media:group.media:description: One survivor, who was not immediately identified by name, was safely guided out of a remote cave in Laos.

item.title: Mount Isa mum calls for more support after birth of premature twins
item.description: Mount Isa mother Kristen Curd spent four months in Townsville after giving birth to premature twin boys, and says the hardest part began when they finally came home.
item.dc:creator: Kavina Kumar

media:group.media:description: Kristen Curd returned to the RFDS in Mount Isa with twins Sam and Ben after four months in Townsville neonatal care.

item.title: Should politicians be using AI-generated images?
item.description: A Victorian state MP's use of an apparently AI-generated image raises questions about whether AI has any place in politics.
item.dc:creator: Jean Bell and Matt Neal

media:group.media:description: Roma Britnell has held the South-West Coast seat for the Liberal Party since 2015.

item.title: Helping 'big personality' marsupial recover after bushfires left them homeless
item.description: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kelly

media:group.media:description: Without a clear picture of the dunnart population before the fire, researchers are now able to monitor the species more closely.

item.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say they have been failed by AFL
item.description: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall and Andy Burns

media:group.media:description: Dustin* loves football but alleged bullying at his local club also made him hate it.

item.title: 'His left side was missing:' How teen survived horrific shark attack
item.description: Fourteen-year-old Sam Nai survived a devastating shark attack with a desperate swim to safety, followed by 20 surgeries to reconstruct his abdomen.
item.dc:creator: Baz Ruddick

media:group.media:description: Sam Nai had weekly surgeries for four months during his recovery.

item.title: Ski resorts remain optimistic despite forecast for warmer winter
item.description: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Mangelsdorf and Philippe Perez

media:group.media:description: Ski resorts are remaining optimistic despite long-term forecasts outlining a warmer winter than normal.

item.title: Australians are watching scrutiny of the Bondi attack through a keyhole
item.description: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.
item.dc:creator: Sean Rubinsztein-Dunlop

media:group.media:description: There was little public challenge to the decisions and testimony of ASIO director-general Mike Burgess, pictured, or NSW Police and AFP counter-terrorism chiefs.

item.title: The nine books our critics couldn't put down in May
item.description: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.
item.dc:creator: Kate Evans, Nicola Heath, Declan Fry, Rosie Ofori Ward, Daniel Herborn, and Ying-Di Yin

media:group.media:description: Memoir, non-fiction and novels are among the best books released in May 2026, as chosen by ABC critics.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

media:group.media:description: A John Farnham musical is coming to Sydney Theatre Company in 2026, and is then expected to tour nationally.

item.title: How women over 60 are still being made the butt of the joke in film
item.description: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Nia Vardalos and Demi Moore were two rare depictions of women over 60.

item.title: Canadian man accused of selling chemical online admits aiding suicide
item.description: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

media:group.media:description: Kenneth Law has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of aiding suicide at a court in Ontario.
